introduction.objective: To facilitate the design of various types of assessments on different subject domains using various types of questions.

steps: User clicks 'Add Assessment' button
System shows entry fields for Assessment Title and Assessment Objective
User enters assessment title and objective and submits
System saves inputs and generates unique ID for the assessment
User clicks 'Add Description' button
System shows entry fields with 'Add Field' and 'Image Upload' facility
User adds text entry fields, enters content, uploads images and saves
System stores inputs with the assessment
User clicks 'Add Instruction' button
System shows entry fields with 'Add Field' and 'Image Upload' facility
User adds text entry fields, enters content, uploads images and saves
System stores inputs with the assessment

rules: After going live, user cannot directly edit Assessment Title. An edit request is sent to Admin for approval.
steps: User clicks 'Edit Assessment' button
System shows list of Assessments
User selects Assessment to be edited
System shows edit fields
User edits and submits
System saves updated inputs

rules: After going live, user cannot directly delete Assessment. An edit request is sent to Admin for approval.
steps: User selects 'Delete Assessment' button
System shows list of Assessments
User selects Assessment and clicks 'Delete Request' button
System asks user to confirm
User confirms
System sends 'Request to delete category' message to Admin
If user clicks 'Cancel': System cancels the request
System shows updated category list

variable_structure_definition.description: An assessment has various sections, each represented by Assessment Variables. Each section has subsections or sub-variables.
rules: Rule 1: Maximum of four variable levels in the variable structure
Rule 2: Questions shall be attached only to the last level

hierarchy: First-level variable is parent of 2nd, 3rd, and 4th levels
Second-level variable is parent of 3rd and 4th levels
Third-level variable is parent of 4th level
Under 1st, 2nd and 3rd levels there shall be multiple variables (upside-down tree structure)
variable_structure_rules.note: User may apply four-level, three-level, two-level or one-level variable structure. All sub-variables and questions are tagged under their parent variable.

rules: Question level scores are added up for Level4-Summary Score
Level4-summary scores are added up for Level3-Summary Score
Level3-summary scores are added up for Level2-Summary Score
Level2-summary scores are added up for Level1-Summary Score
score_summation_rules.note: Any of these summary scores may be called for report generation.

steps: User clicks 'Variable Structure' button
System shows entry fields for specifying number of variables (Level1)
User enters number of variables
System shows entry fields for names of Level1 Variables
User enters names of Level1 Variables
System shows entry fields to specify number of levels for each Level1 Variable
User enters number of levels for each variable
System shows Level1 Variables with entry fields for subvariables as per levels specified
User enters names of subvariables under each Level1 Variable
System saves variables, subvariables and their names
User clicks 'Assign Question' button
System shows list of variables and subvariables
User selects last-level variable of each Level1 Variable for question assignment
System presents filter facility for drawing questions from QB
User applies filters
System shows list of questions as per filter
User clicks on a question to open it
System presents full-view of question with all parameters (psychometric values, SME inputs, review comments, question solution)
User examines question and clicks 'Select' or 'Cancel'
If selected: question gets attached to selected last-level variable
System returns to list of filtered questions
User continues selection for all last-level variables
System displays total number of questions against each level post assignment

setting_delivery_count.description: User specifies number of questions to be presented during assessment delivery for each level.
rules: Delivery Count should NOT be less than the Assigned Count
steps: User clicks 'Delivery Count' button
System shows entry field against each last-level variable with Assigned Count
User enters delivery count for each last-level variable and saves
System displays variable with Assigned Count and Delivery Count
setting_delivery_count.note: For randomized delivery, assigned count should be greater than delivery count.

steps: User clicks 'Set Rating-Type Questions' button
System displays list of psychometric-type questions assigned for the assessment
User clicks 'Apply Rating Scale' button
System asks user to specify number of rating points on the scale
User specifies number of rating points
System displays scale with specified rating points and two entry fields for rating value and legend for each point
User enters required inputs and submits
System applies rating scale to selected questions
rating_questions.note: User can continue selecting more sets of questions and assign different rating values and captions.

paired_type_questions.description: Two questions belonging to two different variables are paired together and displayed as one question. Test taker selects one of the pair; scoring based on selection.
steps: User clicks 'Set Paired-Type' button
System displays list of psychometric-type questions assigned for the assessment
User clicks 'Set Pair' button
System shows template where user can drag and drop questions to be paired
User drags and drops questions to be paired from selected list
System shows two entry fields to enter scores based on selection
User enters score values for selected question and non-selected question and submits
System saves the values

rank_type_questions.description: Two or more questions from different variables are grouped together. Test taker ranks each question in order of preference; scoring based on rank assigned.
steps: User clicks 'Set Rank-Type' button
System displays list of psychometric-type questions assigned for the assessment
User clicks 'Set Rank' button
System asks to specify number of questions to be grouped
User specifies the number
System shows template where user can drag and drop questions to be ranked
User drags and drops questions from selected list
System shows entry fields for specifying score value for each rank
User enters score values against each rank and submits
System saves grouping with score inputs

rank_rate_questions.description: Combines ranking with rating. Group of questions is ranked first, then rated. Combines Section 4.2.3 (ranking) and 4.2.1 (rating).
steps: User clicks 'Set Rank-Rate Type' button
System displays list of psychometric-type questions
System shows entry fields to specify number of questions to be grouped
User specifies the number
System shows template for drag and drop ranking
User drags and drops questions from selected list
System shows entry fields for score value for each rank
User enters score values against each rank and submits
System saves grouping with score inputs
User clicks 'Apply Rating Scale' button
System asks user to specify number of rating points on the scale
User specifies number of rating points
System displays scale with entry fields for rating value and legend for each point
User enters required inputs and submits
System applies rating scale to the ranking questions
rank_rate_questions.scoring_formula: Final score = rank value × rating value
rank_rate_questions.example: If end user selects first question from a 4-question group, assigns Rank 1 (score=4) and Rating 5 (from 5-point scale): final score = 4 × 5 = 20

display_interval_setting.description: User sets time-interval for display-based questions (paragraph/image display followed by questions; flash display of words/images followed by questions).
steps: User selects display-based questions from original list
System shows entry field for time interval for each question
User enters time interval and saves
System saves time interval against each question

setting_question_scores.note: Psychometric questions are excluded from this list as their scoring is set differently.
steps: User clicks 'Set Score' button
System shows all questions assigned with their variable information
User selects a question from the list
System shows entry field to add score against each option (incorrect options given zero or negative scores)
User assigns score values and saves
System stores score values against question options
rules: If no value is assigned, default values are: 1 for correct option and 0 for incorrect options

order_of_delivery.description: User sets order in which questions are delivered. Can be set at Level1, Level2, Level3, Level4, and/or question level.
rules: If order is not set at any level, default delivery mode will be random
steps: User clicks 'Set Order of Delivery' button
System shows variables and their levels
User specifies display order for one or more levels
System stores specified order for assessment delivery
display_order_rules: Only Level1 ordered: order applied to Level1 only; subvariables and questions random within Level1
Level1 and Level2 ordered: order applied to Level1 and Level2; lower levels random within Level2
Level1, Level2, Level3 ordered: order applied to these three; lower levels random within Level3
Level1-4 ordered: order applied to all four levels; questions random within Level4
Level1-4 plus Question Level ordered: order applied to all levels and questions; questions displayed in specified order

timed_delivery.description: User sets time allotted for display at Assessment level or any variable/question level.
rules: Timer can be set at only ONE of the levels
steps: User clicks 'Set Timer' button
System shows variables, questions and their levels (Level0 = Assessment Level)
User selects one level for setting time duration
System saves timer inputs
timer_expiry_rules: Level0 (Assessment Level): Test finishes at expiry. End-user can divide time across variables at convenience.
Level1: Each Level1 variable gets time duration; section finishes at expiry. End-user can divide across Level2/3/4.
Level2: Each Level2 variable gets time duration; section disappears at expiry. End-user can divide across Level3/4.
Level3: Each Level3 variable gets time duration; section disappears at expiry. End-user can divide across Level4.
Level4: Each Level4 variable gets time duration; section disappears at expiry. End-user can divide across questions.
Question Level: Each question gets time duration; question disappears at expiry of respective duration.

navigation_rules.description: User sets limits on navigation across sections or questions during assessment delivery.

options.name: Free Navigation and Response Review
options.description: End user can navigate backward and forward as he wishes and review already attempted questions or attempt any question from any succeeding section.

options.name: Only Previous Section Backward Navigation
options.description: End user can navigate only to just previous section and no further backward. Can review already attempted questions of previous section only.

options.name: No Backward/Forward Navigation (Section Level)
options.description: End user cannot navigate to any previous or succeeding sections, and cannot review already attempted or attempt from succeeding sections.

options.name: No Backward/Forward Navigation (Question Level)
options.description: Do not allow movement backward and forward across sections or questions.

multiple_attempt_rules.description: User sets limits on number of attempts the end-user is allowed.

options.name: Multiple Retakes Allowed
options.description: End user can retake same assessment any number of times.

options.name: Multiple Retakes Not Allowed
options.description: End user can take assessment only once.

options.name: Multiple Sessions Allowed
options.description: End user can break session at any point and renew at a later time.

options.name: Multiple Sessions Not Allowed
options.description: End-user must finish assessment in single go. No break allowed. If break occurs, must freshly start another session.

multiple_attempt_rules.note: Fresh session needs Admin approval, or automatic mechanism based on incomplete session condition.
